steps.title: Set up the probability
steps.content: $P(\text{at least }45)=\dfrac{\text{members aged}\ge 45}{\text{total members}}$. The total is $135$, the denominator for every choice.

steps.title: Read the count from the table
steps.content:
Adding the rows for ages $45$ and older (north and south of Center St.) gives $110$ members. So the probability is $\dfrac{110}{135}$.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
